steps.title: Spot the contrast between appearance and proven reality
steps.content: The method "seems rudimentary today," yet the 2015 study shows it was remarkably accurate—this opposition between initial impression and factual finding is the decisive clue.

steps.title: Apply the concession signal
steps.content:
The logical relationship is concession: the method seems rudimentary today, yet the 2015 study shows it was remarkably accurate — accepting the first claim while pivoting to evidence that undercuts it. The blank needs a transition signaling that concession.
  • Choice A — "As such,": signals a logical result or consequence, so fails — the accuracy finding is not a consequence of seeming rudimentary. ✗
  • Choice B — "That said,": signals concession — it acknowledges the preceding point and introduces a qualifying contrast, fitting perfectly. ✓
  • Choice C — "In other words,": signals restatement or clarification, so fails — the second sentence introduces new evidence, not a rephrasing. ✗
  • Choice D — "For example,": signals an illustration of the preceding claim, so fails — the 2015 finding contradicts rather than exemplifies "rudimentary." ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
